sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or his designee to amend Contract No.
105921-1 with TruePoint Solutions, LLC for professional services, increasing the amount by
$180,000, for a total not-to-exceed amount of $1,477,200 and extending the term by two
years for the term beginning June 1, 2015 to June 30, 2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the additional professional services are available in the Fiscal Year (FY) 2026
Permit Service Center Fund, Sewer Fund, and General Fund and in the Fiscal Year (FY)
2027 Permit Service Center Fund, Sewer Fund, and General Fund, as itemized below, and
any future expenditures are subject to Council approval of the proposed citywide budget and
Annual Appropriations Ordinances.
FY 2026: Professional Services – Planning
$30,000 Fund 621 - Permit Service Center Fund
FY 2026: Professional Services – Public Works
$50,000 Fund 611 - Sewer Fund, Public Works
$25,000 Fund 011 - General Fund, Public Works
FY 2027: Professional Services – Public Works
$50,000 Fund 611 - Sewer Fund, Public Works
$25,000 Fund 011 - General Fund, Public Works
$180,000 Total Professional Services
sections.current_situation:
The departments of Planning & Development and Public Works currently use the Accela
Civic Platform as their permitting system to process permit applications, schedule
inspections, receive permit application payments online, and maintain permits. TruePoint
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099

Contract No. 105921-1 Amendment: TruePoint Solutions, LLC for Professional Services CONSENT CALENDAR
February 25, 2025
Solutions provides the professional services necessary to effectively implement and
maintain these critical features and functionalities, as well as to provide consultation and
technical support. The contract amendment is essential to continue the successful operation
of this enterprise permitting software that is used on a daily basis by the Planning &
Development and Public Works departments, and the members of the public.
This contract extension aligns with the City’s strategic goal to be a customer-focused
organization that provides excellent, timely and easily accessible service to the community.
sections.background:
Established in 2004, TruePoint Solutions, LLC provides design, implementation, and support
services to public sector technology solutions. In 2005, TruePoint Solutions became an
Accela-Certified implementation partner.
In 2015, the City contracted with TruePoint Solutions for report writing and additional
scripting and configuration of Accela’s Building Permit system. In 2016, the City Council
approved an amendment with TruePoint Solutions for the management of the Private Sewer
Laterals module for the Department of Public Works. In 2018, the City Council approved an
amendment with TruePoint Solutions to provide professional assistance for the work on
system issues as requested and prioritized by the City and suggest additional
enhancements/improvements.
The City has enhanced Accela with the help of TruePoint Solutions to streamline internal
processes, enhance online functionality, and make services more accessible to the
community. Recent examples of the additional functionality improvements include the
adding of the following services online:
 Pay for Public Works permits;
 Applications for street events;
 Application for oversized load permit;
 Application for private sewer lateral (PSL) certification;
 PSL enforcement documentation; and
 The ability to look up and save PSL certificates.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The City will help reduce carbon emissions by allowing most, if not all, permitting processes
to be performed online thereby reducing traffic into the City. Further, the online application
capability of the system eliminates the use of paper thus working towards the City’s goal of
achieving zero waste to landfills.
sections.rationale:
TruePoint Solutions is an Accela-Certified implementation partner. TruePoint Solutions
performs specialized process analysis, configuration, and scripting to support the City's

Contract No. 105921-1 Amendment: TruePoint Solutions, LLC for Professional Services CONSENT CALENDAR
February 25, 2025
permitting system. TruePoint Solutions has in-depth knowledge of City business processes
and data architecture that would take another vendor years to acquire.
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered completing these projects and enhancements without professional
services; however, doing so would lead to an inefficient allocation of staff resources and
significantly extend implementation timelines.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, 510-981-6541
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 105921-1 AMENDMENT: TRUEPOINT SOLUTIONS, LLC FOR
ACCELA PROFESSIONAL SERVICES
WHEREAS, TruePoint Solutions provides design, implementation, and support services
to public sector technology solutions, and is an Accela-Certified implementation partner;
and
WHEREAS, TruePoint Solutions is essential to continue the successful operation of the
Planning and Public Works departments’ permitting software and meet ongoing technical
support requirements; and
WHEREAS, TruePoint Solutions has previously provided excellent report writing,
scripting, and consulting services and therefore has an in-depth knowledge of City
business processes and data architecture that would take another vendor years to
acquire; and
WHEREAS, funding for the additional professional services are available in the Fiscal
Year (FY) 2025 Permit Service Center fund, Sewer Fund, and General Fund and in the
Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 Permit Service Center fund, Sewer Fund, and General Fund, and
any future expenditures are subject to Council approval of the proposed citywide budget
and Annual Appropriations Ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to amend contract No. 105921-1 with TruePoint Solutions,
LLC, increasing the amount by $180,000, for a total not-to-exceed amount of $1,477,200
and for the term beginning June 1, 2015 to June 30, 2027.
